summary: Dual-use bulk email/SMS red-team tool with fully obfuscated runtime
details: Package advertises itself as an 'Advanced email sender' with 'red-team' and 'smtp' keywords. CLI subcommands send bulk SMS via SMTP-to-SMS gateways, send bulk email, scan for open redirects, generate DKIM keys, and extract contacts from authenticated Gmail/Outlook accounts via OAuth. The entire runtime under `.ad/` (46 files including the main entry, license check, contacts extractor, SMS providers, and web command) is shipped as single-line obfuscator.io output with shuffled string arrays, RC4-decoded literals, self-defending wrapper, and debug-protection counters; the build script is `node scripts/obfuscate.js` and `javascript-obfuscator` is listed in devDependencies. Behavior reached at install or require time is limited to a commercial license check that POSTs a user-supplied license key plus HWID/hostname/platform to a hardcoded vendor endpoint and caches the response in `~/.nolimit/activation.json`; no environment-variable scraping, credential-path reads (~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc, browser profiles), or attacker-controlled dropper was observed on the install or import path. All hostile-to-third-parties actions (bulk SMS/email send, contact harvest from third-party mailboxes) are gated behind explicit CLI subcommands an operator chooses to run. The combination of openly hostile-to-third-parties purpose plus pervasive runtime obfuscation makes hidden functionality in non-fully-deobfuscated files a real but unverified concern; routed to human review rather than block because no installer-harm mechanism was demonstrated.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
